meta.title: NVIDIA DSX AI Factory demo lane

thesis.supertrend: AI factory buildout is expanding from chips into full-system delivery: racks, power, cooling, packaging, test, and cloud capacity.
thesis.lane: HBM test and advanced packaging support tools
thesis.thesis_sentence: The lane worth prioritizing is not just AI chips themselves, but the test, inspection, and advanced-packaging support layers that must scale before AI factory capacity can come online.
thesis.bottleneck_call: HBM-related test capacity and advanced-packaging inspection remain critical enabling layers ahead of wider AI factory deployment.
thesis.weakest_assumption: Demand growth in AI servers continues to translate into sustained HBM and advanced packaging capex rather than a short-lived inventory cycle.
thesis.thesis_breaker: If packaging expansion slows or HBM demand normalizes faster than expected, the support-tool layer may de-rate before broader AI factory buildout matures.

bottlenecks.label: HBM test enablement
bottlenecks.why_it_matters: HBM capacity cannot translate into usable supply without probe-card and test throughput at the right yield and timing.

bottlenecks.label: Advanced-packaging inspection and metrology
bottlenecks.why_it_matters: As packaging complexity rises, inspection systems become gating tools before capacity can scale cleanly.

evidence.label: Confirmed
evidence.title: Q1 2026 results
evidence.summary: FormFactor said DRAM revenue reached a record and noted higher HBM application demand, while serving all three major HBM manufacturers in production.

evidence.label: Confirmed
evidence.title: March 2026 Hawk order announcement
evidence.summary: Camtek announced about $25 million of Hawk systems orders for AI applications from an IDM.

evidence.label: Confirmed
evidence.title: Q1 2026 results
evidence.summary: Amkor reported record first-quarter revenue and highlighted AI-driven advanced packaging demand plus continued Arizona expansion.

evidence.label: Confirmed
evidence.title: NVIDIA DSX platform
evidence.summary: NVIDIA positions DSX as a blueprint for full AI factory infrastructure, not just a server product.

evidence.label: Inferred
evidence.title: Advanced packaging market page
evidence.summary: Camtek describes Hawk systems as aimed at advanced packaging use cases including HBM and chiplets, supporting its relevance to AI packaging ramps.

companies.name: FormFactor
companies.notes: Direct exposure to HBM test throughput. Less obvious than memory leaders, but tied to production enablement.

companies.name: Camtek
companies.notes: Inspection and metrology exposure to AI packaging complexity. Smaller and more volatile than broader semiconductor equipment names.

companies.name: Amkor
companies.notes: Not the smallest name, but meaningful leverage to advanced packaging expansion and AI system buildout.

companies.name: Ichor Holdings
companies.notes: Smaller-cap line item with exposure to semiconductor capex. More of a secondary clue name than a first-layer thesis anchor.

companies.name: Aehr Test Systems
companies.notes: Small-cap test exposure with meaningful upside if AI-related test intensity broadens, but higher volatility and narrower evidence base.

companies.name: Ultra Clean Holdings
companies.notes: Secondary semiconductor capital-equipment exposure. Useful as a smaller-cap clue name, not the center of the lane.

catalysts.label: Next quarterly earnings
catalysts.watch_for: Lead-time language, AI demand mix, HBM / advanced packaging commentary

catalysts.label: Packaging capacity expansion updates
catalysts.watch_for: Whether AI packaging investment continues to move from planning into physical deployment

catalysts.label: AI server demand visibility
catalysts.watch_for: Whether cloud and enterprise AI factory demand continues to support packaging and test capex
